So with that in mind id like to hear people's experiences of giving people who dont know eva or even told know anime copies of eva (particularly EoE0 to watch - the reactions and the questions.

[]: Post by Xard » Thu Apr 14, 2011 4:07 pm Q: "why does that girl have blue hair" A: "LOLANIME"

[]: Post by Reichu » Thu Apr 14, 2011 4:11 pm :my dad sees Shinji talking into the green payphone: "Is that a girl?"

[]: Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Apr 14, 2011 4:47 pm Reichu wrote: "Is that a girl?"

[]: Post by Jayfive » Thu Apr 14, 2011 4:59 pm The one friend I lent the first disk to (episodes 1-4) returned it saying it was just a set of excuses for giant robot fights.

[]: Post by Stryker » Thu Apr 14, 2011 5:18 pm I'm thinking of getting my bros back into anime (it all started with [s:hpw8tyll]Princess Mononoke[/s:hpw8tyll] Dragonball Z).

[]: Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Apr 14, 2011 5:26 pm The only real example I have is watching 1.0 in San Fran theaters with my stepsister: She found it interesting, but I guess wasn't interested enough to eventually check out 2.0. She's not as into anime as my stepbrother is, who's familiar with the series (dunno about NME) and feels "it's a really good series". I think once I was watching a part of EoE with my stepmom or dad in the other room, and they were wondering why a feminine voice was screaming so much. Regarding the OP, I was personally on the flip side: When hearing about NGE in high school a friend was commenting how much ass Asuka kicks in EoE (and another friend would sometimes play the MP3 of

Article: Why does that girl have blue hair
 was screaming so much. Regarding the OP, I was personally on the flip side: When hearing about NGE in high school a friend was commenting how much ass Asuka kicks in EoE (and another friend would sometimes play the MP3 of Misato's theme every so often)...I was curious about Eva due to hearing it was psychological, but avoided it due to the mecha fights and thinking Anno was some egoistical asshole (My opinion softened--greatly--upon learning he suffered from Depression with production of the show http://wiki.evageeks.org/User:Sailor_Star_Dust ). Then in College, maybe a few months before I buckled down and watched the series, I asked a friend if the series was any good...They said it was, but told me the following: "In EoE, Shinji says "I'm so fucked up...." It seems that he FINALLY gets it!"

[]: Re: Why does that girl have blue hair? []: Post by Jayfive » Thu Apr 14, 2011 5:59 pm I would love to sit my mates down in front of Eoe - not likely to happen as 1) most of them wouldnt watch a 'cartoon' and 2) you really need to have watched the series first. Someone really should do one of those reaction vids like with The Maze of 2G1C for at least the opening scene of EoE. 'What is he....OH DEAR GOD WHY?'

[]: Post by Cody MacArthur Fett » Thu Apr 14, 2011 6:09 pm Jayfive wrote: That reminds me, i told my friend very little about the show - i was even going to give her just the discs without the sleeve or booklet. I did explain though that theres a certain commitment involved in watching the series to the end and that anime is more 'show, dont tell' than western sci-fi and..well...western cinema in general.

[]: Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Apr 14, 2011 6:21 pm Cody MacArthur Fett wrote: My mom and aunt then proceeded to have a 15+ minute long conversation on the merits of Shinji's character. I honestly wasn't expecting that.

[]: Post by esselfortium » Thu Apr 14, 2011 6:25 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: I was curious about Eva due to hearing it was psychological, but avoided it due to the mecha fights and thinking Anno was some egoistical asshole

[]: Post by symbv » Thu Apr 14, 2011 6:26 pm Perhaps because they wanted to see more kids in the west to be like Shinji?

[]: Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Apr 14, 2011 6:52 pm esselfortium wrote: Weeeeeelllllllll.....

[]: Post by violaine » Thu Apr 14, 2011 9:53 pm on evangelion, vol 1's cover: girl:"So, is that a girl or a boy?" me:"*laughs* It's a guy." other girl:"No, wait, give me that. I can tell. ... It's a girl. Look at that. *points to chest*" me:"no, the plug suit just makes it look like he has tits---" other girl:"I know a girl when I see one, sweetie."

[]: Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Apr 14, 2011 10:06 pm Poor, poor, baka -Shinji and his Nadia-looks.

Article: Why does that girl have blue hair
[]: Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Apr 14, 2011 10:06 pm Poor, poor, baka -Shinji and his Nadia-looks.

[]: Post by Trajan » Thu Apr 14, 2011 10:35 pm LOL My dad never mistook Shinji for a girl but he got Maya and Shinji confused for most of the series. My dad and my sister have both seen it: they have a few things in common regarding their opinions.. 1) They hate Asuka. 2) They love Kaji. 3) They like Rei's blue hair. 4) EoE made them sad. 5) They think Shinji and Kaworu should become life partners.

[]: Post by Bomby von Bombsville » Thu Apr 14, 2011 11:30 pm "So this Japanese animation you're watching, is it just a bunch of kids shows?" -My dad, seeing me on my laptop the night I started watching Evangelion. Weird to think that I was actually still living with my parents at that time.

[]: Post by zuka » Fri Apr 15, 2011 12:14 am Cody MacArthur Fett wrote: As myself, I have currently only shown Eva to my family members, and here were their reactions. My dad really liked it (Rebuild 1.11, that is), called it an interesting story, and apparently really related to Gendo.
